title: Abort: double-free in `_PyPegen_raise_error_known_location` (`pegen_errors.c:363`)
description: `Parser/pegen_errors.c`: `_PyPegen_raise_error_known_location` passes `error_line` to `Py_BuildValue` via the reference-stealing `N` format (L346). `Py_BuildValue` consumes the `N` arg even on failure, but the `error:` path still runs `Py_XDECREF(error_line)` (L363) -- a double-free. On `Py_REF_DEBUG` builds the second decref drives the refcount negative and aborts at `Include/refcount.h:520`.
notes: Double-free, not an uninitialized field. error_line is handed to Py_BuildValue("(OnnNnn)", ..., error_line, ...) at pegen_errors.c:346 with the reference-stealing 'N' format; Py_BuildValue consumes the 'N' arg even when it fails (do_mktuple/do_ignore in Python/modsupport.c, see comment at modsupport.c:263). Under OOM PyTuple_New(6) (or an earlier conversion) fails, dropping error_line's only ref; Py_BuildValue returns NULL, the function does `goto error`, and Py_XDECREF(error_line) at L363 decrefs the freed object a second time. The _Py_NegativeRefcount check is gated on Py_REF_DEBUG, so it aborts on both debug builds (ft_debug_asan and jit) at start=54, same site; release builds (ft_release/upstream) compile the check out (n/a) but the double-free is real -- a full set_nomemory sweep segfaulted on upstream. Freed str slab is reused for the pre-allocated MemoryError instance under OOM, hence 'object type name: MemoryError' / 'is freed'. Fix: set error_line=NULL right after the Py_BuildValue call (or use the non-stealing 'O' format). Three vehicles reach the parser error path: two via ast.parse (pyclbr._create_tree), one via multiprocessing.forkserver._handle_preload. Distinct from OOM-0003 (code_dealloc uninitialized _co_unique_id assert).
